chatbot.sectionLabel: Why not just use an AI chatbot?
chatbot.sectionHeading: Quality comes from process — not a single prompt.
chatbot.sectionSub: A general AI assistant can read your notice — but AI systems are known to hallucinate legal sources and miss domain-specific questions. TaxaLex uses five specialized agents that check each other's work.
chatbot.generic1: Generates one draft without any automatic cross-checking or self-review
chatbot.generic2: Does not know which domain-specific legal questions to ask — you need to prompt them
chatbot.generic3: Can hallucinate legal citations and case references — errors go undetected without verification
chatbot.generic4: No perspective test: "How would the authority counter this argument?"
chatbot.generic5: No automatic deadline calculation from the document
chatbot.generic6: Output quality and completeness depend heavily on how the prompt is written
chatbot.agentDrafter: Creates the initial objection using legal domain knowledge (§ AO, § SGG, § BGB, § KSchG)
chatbot.agentReviewer: Checks legal correctness, deadline compliance, and formal requirements
chatbot.agentFactChecker: Verifies cited legal sources and rulings via live search — flags inaccuracies before they reach the final draft
chatbot.agentAdversary: Reviews the draft from the authority's perspective — to identify and address weak points
chatbot.agentConsolidator: Combines all reviews into one structured, clearly reasoned final letter
chatbot.ctaText: See how it works — try the interactive demo, no registration required.

templates.description: Download blank templates for the most common objections in Germany — tax, Jobcenter, rent, employment and more. Or fill them online with AI in minutes.

useCasePage.heroSubtitle: Learn about your rights for the most common German administrative notices — with deadlines, legal basis, and success rates.
useCasePage.bottomSubtitle: Upload any official notice — our AI identifies the type automatically, finds objection grounds, and drafts a professional letter in minutes.

tax.title: Tax Assessment
tax.desc: Received a tax assessment you disagree with? An objection under § 347 AO is the legal mechanism to challenge it within 30 days. Common errors: miscalculated work expenses, rejected special expenses, missing tax credits, or solidarity surcharge errors.
tax.tip: File objection even without grounds — reasoning can be submitted later.

grundsteuer.title: Property Tax (2022 Reform)
grundsteuer.desc: Since the 2022 property tax reform, millions of property owners received new assessments. Common errors: wrong land area, incorrect Bodenrichtwert, wrong usage classification, or incorrect building data. The error rate in new assessments is high — worth challenging.
grundsteuer.success: Very high success rate with provable data errors
grundsteuer.tip: Compare property data with land register — many errors immediately visible.

jobcenter.title: Jobcenter / Bürgergeld
jobcenter.desc: Jobcenter rejected or reduced your Bürgergeld benefits? An objection under § 78 SGG is free of charge and often suspends enforcement. Common cases: unlawful sanctions, incorrect needs calculation, uncounted allowances, rejected moving costs.
jobcenter.success: Free of charge under § 184 SGG — no cost risk
jobcenter.tip: Objection generally suspends enforcement — benefits continue to be paid.

rente.title: Pension Notice
rente.desc: Your pension was calculated too low? The Deutsche Rentenversicherung bases calculations on insurance periods and contribution points. Errors occur through missing insurance periods, incorrect child-rearing credits, or uncounted training periods.
rente.success: Often successful with provable missing insurance periods
rente.tip: Always worth checking — every euro adds up over decades of retirement.

krankenversicherung.title: Health Insurance
krankenversicherung.desc: Health insurer rejected coverage for medication, therapy, or medical aids? You have the right to object under § 78 SGG. The insurer must decide within 3–5 weeks. Common grounds: insufficient assessment of medical necessity, defective MDK report.
krankenversicherung.success: Approx. 40% success rate with well-reasoned objection
krankenversicherung.tip: Medical certificate of necessity significantly increases success chances.

kuendigung.title: Dismissal
kuendigung.desc: Received a dismissal notice you believe is unjust or defective? Employment protection law shields workers in companies with 10+ employees after 6 months. Grounds for invalidity: lack of social justification, missing works council hearing, procedural defects.
kuendigung.deadline: 3 weeks to file at labor court
kuendigung.success: Many cases result in a severance settlement
kuendigung.tip: ⚠ Only 3 weeks! Act immediately — after that the dismissal is final.

miete.title: Rent Increase / Deposit
miete.desc: Landlord demanding a rent increase or withholding your deposit? Rent increases are only valid up to the local reference rent and within the statutory cap (§ 558 Abs. 3 BGB). Deposits must be returned after you move out within a reasonable timeframe.
miete.deadline: 2 months to object to rent increase
miete.success: Often successful when local rent index is exceeded
miete.tip: Check local rent index — many increases exceed the permitted level.

bussgeld.title: Fine / Penalty Notice
bussgeld.desc: Received a fine for speeding, using a phone, running a red light, or parking violations? An objection under § 67 OWiG entitles you to court proceedings and file access. Common grounds: measurement errors, expired calibration, defective driver identification.
bussgeld.success: Approx. 30% of cases dropped after file inspection
bussgeld.tip: Always request file access first — often reveals procedural defects.

howItWorksPage.heroSubtitle: Three simple steps. Five AI agents. One ready-to-send objection letter.
howItWorksPage.formatPdf: PDF – Direct text extraction or OCR
howItWorksPage.formatImg: JPG, PNG – Photo of the notice (OCR)
howItWorksPage.formatNote: Max. 10 MB per file. Multiple files allowed.
howItWorksPage.extractionDesc: TaxaLex automatically extracts key data such as tax office name, notice date, assessment amounts, deadlines and legal references. You review and confirm the extracted data before generation begins.
howItWorksPage.step2Subtitle: Claude, Gemini and Perplexity work together
howItWorksPage.analysisNote: The entire analysis typically completes in under 5 minutes. You can watch the progress in real time.
howItWorksPage.step3Subtitle: Review, download, and send to the authority
howItWorksPage.legalNoteDesc: The generated document is an AI-assisted draft. It is not legal advice within the meaning of the German Legal Services Act (RDG). For complex cases, consult a tax advisor or lawyer before submission.

drafter.description: Creates the initial objection draft including all formal requirements, citations of legal basis, and argumentation structure.

reviewer.description: Reviews the draft for logical errors, missing arguments, incorrect citations, and formal deficiencies.

factChecker.description: Verifies all legal citations against current legislation and recent court rulings via Perplexity search.

adversary.description: Takes the authority's perspective and identifies potential weaknesses in the objection — so they can be addressed proactively.

consolidator.description: Merges the outputs of all four agents into a final, polished objection letter ready for submission.

expatsPage.heroTitle: Navigate German bureaucracy — in your language.
expatsPage.heroSubtitle: Received a German notice you don't understand? TaxaLex analyses it, explains it in your language, and drafts a professional German objection letter — in minutes.
expatsPage.feature1Desc: Use TaxaLex in English, German, Turkish, Russian, Polish, Ukrainian, or Arabic. The objection letter is always in German — required by German authorities.
expatsPage.feature2Desc: Your objection letter is drafted in legally correct German — even if you don't speak German. You can download an English translation for your reference.
expatsPage.feature3Desc: German objection deadlines are typically 30 days. TaxaLex helps you act fast — the AI drafts your letter in under 5 minutes so you never miss a deadline.
expatsPage.feature4Desc: All legal references, deadlines, and authority addresses are specific to German law: § 347 AO, SGG § 84, BGB § 541, and more.
expatsPage.useCasesSubtitle: These are the notices expats in Germany most frequently receive and need to appeal.
expatsPage.languagesSubtitle: Switch the interface language in the footer at any time. The objection letter is always submitted in German.

contactPage.title: Contact
contactPage.subtitle: Questions, feedback, or issues with your appeal? We're here to help.
contactPage.legalNote: {brandName} does not provide legal advice within the meaning of the Legal Services Act (RDG). For urgent legal matters, please consult a licensed attorney or tax advisor.

pricingPage.heroSubtitle: Pay per case or subscribe for unlimited access. No hidden fees.
pricingPage.addonDesc: Have your AI-generated letter reviewed by a licensed tax advisor or lawyer before sending.
pricingPage.legalNoteText: TaxaLex generates AI-assisted draft objection letters. These are not legal advice within the meaning of the German Legal Services Act (RDG). Professional review is a quality check by a licensed professional — not a legal representation mandate.

templatesPage.heroSubtitle: Download professionally structured templates for the most common administrative objections in Germany — or fill them online with your details and let our AI complete the rest.
templatesPage.infoBannerTip: Tip: Use our AI to generate a personalised version — upload your notice and get a letter tailored to your exact case.
templatesPage.bottomTitle: Don't see your case? Let AI handle it.
templatesPage.bottomSubtitle: Upload any official notice — tax, social, employment, or housing — and our AI will analyse it, identify objection grounds, and draft a professional letter in minutes.

steuerbescheid-einspruch.title: Tax Assessment Objection
steuerbescheid-einspruch.desc: Formal objection against an income tax, trade tax, or property tax assessment under § 347 AO. Includes legal basis, objection grounds, and request for suspension of enforcement.

grundsteuer-einspruch.title: Property Tax Objection (2022 Reform)
grundsteuer-einspruch.desc: Challenge your new property tax assessment following the 2022 Grundsteuer reform. Covers errors in land area, Bodenrichtwert, and usage classification.

jobcenter-widerspruch.title: Jobcenter Objection (Bürgergeld)
jobcenter-widerspruch.desc: Object to rejected or reduced Bürgergeld (citizen income) payments, sanctions, or benefit cuts. Free of charge under § 184 SGG.

rente-widerspruch.title: Pension Notice Objection
rente-widerspruch.desc: Dispute incorrect pension calculations from Deutsche Rentenversicherung — pension points, insurance periods, child-rearing credits.

krankenkasse-widerspruch.title: Health Insurance Coverage Objection
krankenkasse-widerspruch.desc: Challenge your GKV insurer's refusal to cover medication, therapy, medical aids, or specialist referrals. References medical necessity (§ 12 SGB V).

pflegegrad-widerspruch.title: Nursing Care Level Objection
pflegegrad-widerspruch.desc: Object to an incorrect nursing care level (Pflegegrad 1–5) assessment by the MDK or Medicproof. Includes points breakdown and appeal of assessment criteria.

elterngeld-widerspruch.title: Parental Benefit / Child Benefit Objection
elterngeld-widerspruch.desc: Challenge incorrect calculation or rejection of Elterngeld, ElterngeldPlus, or Kindergeld. References §§ 1–4 BEEG and applicable income calculation rules.

kuendigung-widerspruch.title: Dismissal Objection / Employment Protection
kuendigung-widerspruch.desc: Object to unfair or socially unjustified dismissal. Includes demand for revocation, notice of employment protection claim, and reference to § 4 KSchG deadline.

mieterhöhung-widerspruch.title: Rent Increase Objection
mieterhöhung-widerspruch.desc: Challenge unjustified rent increases above the local reference rent (Mietspiegel), violations of the 20% cap rule, or missing formal requirements.

nebenkosten-widerspruch.title: Ancillary Cost Statement Objection
nebenkosten-widerspruch.desc: Contest incorrect utility cost statements: missing receipts, wrong distribution keys, late delivery, or non-chargeable cost items.

maengelanzeige.title: Defect Notice to Landlord
maengelanzeige.desc: Formal written notice of apartment defects (mould, heating failure, water damage) with deadline for repair and reference to rent reduction rights under § 536 BGB.

bussgeld-einspruch.title: Traffic Fine Objection
bussgeld-einspruch.desc: Challenge speed camera fines, parking tickets, and driving bans. Covers measurement errors, procedural defects, and driver identification issues.

ruecktritt-kaufvertrag.title: Contract Withdrawal (Defective Goods)
ruecktritt-kaufvertrag.desc: Withdraw from a purchase contract due to defects after failed repair attempts. References §§ 437, 440 BGB and sets a final repair deadline.

gez-widerspruch.title: Broadcasting Fee Objection (GEZ/ARD-ZDF)
gez-widerspruch.desc: Object to broadcasting fee assessments: incorrect number of households, exemption eligibility (Bürgergeld recipients, disabled persons), or duplicate billing.

akteneinsicht.title: Request for File Access
akteneinsicht.desc: Formal request to inspect your administrative file under § 29 VwVfG or § 25 SGB X. Standard for use before filing an objection to understand the authority's reasoning.

zahlungsaufforderung.title: Payment Demand / Formal Notice
zahlungsaufforderung.desc: Professional payment reminder with legal default notice, interest claim under § 288 BGB, and deadline before legal action. Suitable for private and commercial claims.

templateDetailPage.aiUpsellTitle: Let AI personalise it for you
templateDetailPage.aiUpsellDesc: Upload your official notice — our AI analyses it and fills in all fields based on your actual document in seconds.
templateDetailPage.legalNote: This template is provided for informational purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice. Please review before submission.
